Introducing the Gemini 2.5 Computer Use model

design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed derived from title+kind+audience+generated_at to select workflow-command-center hero, Outfit typography, gapless-bento-grid + decision-matrix + source-card-dossier components, GSAP paradigms image-scale-fade-scroll and hover-physics. AIDA Check: clear hero attention, proof via sources, desire via workflow guide, action via CTA. Font Selection: Outfit (primary) with system fallbacks. Hero Math Verification: clamp sizing yields 2–3 line H1 at 1120px max-width. Bento Density Verification: CSS includes grid-auto-flow: dense on .bento-grid. GSAP Motion Selection: guarded GSAP fades and hover physics. Label Sweep & Button Check: black pill primary CTA and high-contrast secondary CTA verified.
headline: Personal AI Agent Market Brief — What Matters Now for Buyers and Builders

site_brief.audience: buyers and builders tracking personal AI agents
site_brief.goal: Create a frequently refreshed editorial blog homepage with a long-form lead analysis, practical how-to explainer, source cards, and a clear Super perspective on why computer-use caching makes repeated agent work cheaper and better. Make it read like a useful market briefing, not a list of summaries.
site_brief.title: Personal AI Agent Market Brief

summary: A field‑tested market briefing for teams tracking personal AI agents and computer‑use assistants. This page synthesizes recent news with practical guidance, compares leading assistants like ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, Siri, Folk, and Orchids, and explains why Super’s reusable computer‑use cache changes the economics of repeated workflows.
